name: File upload
goal: Provide a drag-and-drop dropzone that collects a File[] selection and emits a ce-files event.
description: Static-by-default dropzone with click-to-browse + drag-over highlight + drop handling. The component itself never uploads anything — it only collects a File[] from the native <input type=file> or a DragEvent and emits ce-files. The parent owns transport. Default state is an idle, idiomatic zone with a sensible label and hint (CDR-007); attributes accept, multiple, name, label, hint, disabled are all optional. Multimodal chat composers, profile-photo pickers, and report attach surfaces are the primary use cases.

props.name: accept
props.description: MIME / extension filter forwarded to the native <input> (e.g. 'image/*', '.csv,.tsv').

props.name: multiple
props.description: Allow selecting / dropping more than one file. Drops are truncated to one when off.

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name; echoed in ce-files detail.

props.name: label
props.description: Primary headline shown in the zone.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables clicks, keyboard, drag-over, and drop.

events.name: ce-files
events.description: Fired whenever the user picks or drops a file (or files when multiple is set).

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Leading icon glyph or component. Defaults to ⬆.

slots.name: label
slots.description: Overrides the label attribute when used.

slots.name: hint
slots.description: Overrides the hint attribute when used.
Body content below the zone (a rendered file list, validation messages, ...).

cssVariables.name: --ce-space-2
cssVariables.description: Vertical gap between rows + body offset.

sideEffects.description: Triggers the native file picker dialog on click or Enter/Space.
sideEffects.reason: Dropzone affordance — the user activated the control.

a11y.notes: Zone has role=button and is keyboard-activatable via Enter/Space. aria-labelledby points to the visible label, aria-disabled mirrors the disabled prop, and the native <input type=file> remains in the DOM (visually hidden) so screen readers still see a file control.
